DISCOVERY 301, DEFENDANT YAN EPSHTEIN'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF BO YUAN'S RESPONSES TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS TO PLAINTIFF AND PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF DID NOT TIMELY RESPOND AND HAS WAIVED OBJECTIONS. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE VERIFIED, OBJECTION-FREE RESPONSES WITHIN THREE WEEKS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER AND TO PAY SANCTIONS OF $1590 TO DEFENDANT WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Plaintiff's counsel is sanctioned $400.00 for failure to appear. The Order to Show Cause is re-set to April 07,2026, at 10:30am in Department 610. Judge: Rochelle East; Clerk Z. Dizon; Proceedings not reported. (610)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-10-2025 Continued To Feb-03-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Compel Plaintiff Bo Yuan?S Responses To Written Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions To Plaintiff And Plaintiff?S Counsel; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Jared Mainini, Esq.; And [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Dec-18-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT YAN EPSHTEIN'S Motion For Order To Compel Plaintiff Bo Yuan's Responses To Written Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions To Plaintiff And Plaintiff's Counsel.

Defendant Yan Epshtein's unopposed motion to compel responses to his first set of special interrogatories and first set of requests for production of documents is granted. Plaintiff did not timely respond and has waived objections. Plaintiff is ordered to provide verified, objection-free responses within three weeks of notice of entry of this order and to pay sanctions of $1590 to defendant within 30 days of notice of entry of this order.

order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
